reasoning: The finding is confirmed real. Full code path verified: (1) design_custom_css is saved via register_setting with sanitize_settings() as callback (setup.php:171), but sanitize_settings() (lines 277-396) never touches design_custom_css — it is passed through array_merge() unsanitized. (2) The sink is functions.php:1074-1075: echo esc_html($options['design_custom_css']) inside a <style> block opened at line 908 and closed at line 1078, rendered on every login page via add_action('login_head', ...) registered in advanced-google-recaptcha.php:182. (3) esc_html() encodes only <, >, &, ", ' — all CSS syntax characters ({, }, ;, :, (, ), @, /) pass through unchanged, so arbitrary CSS rules are injectable. (4) Reachability is correctly stated: only manage_options (administrator) can save these settings (admin.php:278 checks current_user_can('manage_options')). (5) Severity is correctly low: CSS injection without JS is not XSS in any modern browser (expression() is IE6-only), output is limited to the login page style block, and WordPress admins already have arbitrary code execution capability via plugin/theme installs — this does not cross any privilege boundary. The finding's technical description is accurate.
